items.subject: OT Network Design
items.body_preview:
Guys

Given the network design is apparently underway, can I please get visibility of what allowances; if any; have been made in respect a view to future migration from existing BMS philosophy [i.e. UPS connected to BMS via RS485] to something on market
items.ai_summary: Rhys Jones from Galaxy Data Centers is requesting visibility into the network design currently in progress, specifically asking whether the design accounts for future flexibility to migrate from the existing Building Management System (BMS) architecture that relies on RS485 connections to UPS systems toward more modern market alternatives. This matters because locking into legacy BMS connectivity could create technical debt and limit Galaxy's ability to upgrade infrastructure or adopt newer, more efficient monitoring and control systems as technology evolves.
ai_next_steps: Have the network design team provide Rhys with documentation showing what future-proofing provisions (if any) have been built into the current design regarding BMS/UPS connectivity flexibility
